source: Representative walkthrough constructed from public Mr-Robot write-up structure (robots.txt wordlist -> WordPress weak-cred brute -> theme-editor webshell -> SUID nmap root). Author-constructed for offline Phase-1 mapping measurement; NOT a live capture.
vulnhub_correspondence: robots.txt leaks fsocity.dic + key-1; /license base64 leaks WordPress creds (elliot); wpscan dictionary brute of wp-login succeeds; wp-admin theme editor injects PHP for a daemon webshell; /home/robot/key-2 gated by robot's MD5 (cracked offline); su robot; SUID nmap --interactive escalates to root; key-3.

steps.actor_intent: Scan the target with nmap to enumerate open services and versions.
steps.tool_output:
PORT    STATE  SERVICE  VERSION
22/tcp  closed ssh
80/tcp  open   http     Apache httpd
443/tcp open   ssl/http Apache httpd

steps.actor_intent: Fingerprint the web technology stack to identify the CMS.
steps.tool_output: http://10.10.10.5 [200 OK] Apache, HTTPServer[Apache], JQuery, MetaGenerator[WordPress 4.3.1], PoweredBy[WordPress], WordPress[4.3.1], X-Pingback[/xmlrpc.php]

steps.actor_intent: Retrieve robots.txt to discover hidden or disallowed paths.
Read the first key file advertised in robots.txt.
Fetch the /license page and decode the embedded base64 blob, which leaks WordPress credentials.
steps.tool_output:
<!-- do you want a password or something? -->
ZWxsaW90OkVSMjgtMDY1Mgo=
decoded: elliot:ER28-0652

steps.actor_intent: Brute-force the WordPress login form with the leaked username and dictionary.
steps.tool_output:
[SUCCESS] Login successful for elliot
HTTP/1.1 302 Found
Set-Cookie: wordpress_logged_in_abc=elliot%7C...; path=/
Location: /wp-admin/

steps.actor_intent: Use the wp-admin theme editor to inject PHP into 404.php and obtain a webshell.
steps.tool_output:
Theme file 404.php updated.
[+] PHP injected; webshell live at /wp-content/themes/twentyfifteen/404.php
command execution confirmed

steps.actor_intent: Run id through the webshell to confirm command execution and identify the user.
candidate_pool: Run id through the webshell to confirm command execution and identify the user.
Brute-force the WordPress login that has already succeeded.
Fingerprint the HTTP server technology stack again.
Read the root flag despite having no root access yet.

steps.actor_intent: Read robot's MD5 password hash file found in the home directory.
Crack robot's MD5 hash offline with john and the rockyou wordlist.
steps.tool_output:
Loaded 1 password hash (Raw-MD5)
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz (robot)
Session completed
steps.out_of_abstraction_reason: Offline hash cracking (john/hashcat against a leaked OS hash) is local cryptographic work with no HTTP request or web interaction; the 16-action web schema cannot represent it.

steps.actor_intent: Switch to the robot Unix account over the local shell using the cracked password.
steps.out_of_abstraction_reason: A local Unix account switch via su/PAM on an interactive shell is not a web-form or HTTP auth; the schema's auth_attempt/credential_use are web-context only.

steps.actor_intent: Read the second key file now readable as robot.
Enumerate the filesystem for SUID binaries usable for escalation.
Escalate to root by abusing the SUID nmap interactive mode.
steps.tool_output:
Starting nmap V. 3.81 ( Interactive Mode )
# id
uid=1(daemon) gid=1(daemon) euid=0(root)
# whoami
root
candidate_pool: Escalate to root by abusing the SUID nmap interactive mode.
Read the root key file before escalating privileges.

steps.actor_intent: Read the final root key file now that we are root.
